content.full_text:
Farn PolrrrcAt. PnecrrcEs ConaMrssroN
428 J Street. Suite 620. Sacramento, CA g1gl4_232g
(916) 322-5660. Fax (916) 322-0856
September 26,2002
C. April Boling, CPA
7185 Navajo Road, Suite L
San Diego, CA92ll9
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-02-262
Dear Ms. Boling:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of the San Diego
County Republican Central Committee regarding the provisions of the Political Reform
Act (the "Act").I
QUESTTON
Do expenditures that constitute "mernber communications" under section 85312
by a political parfi committee nonetheless trigger a pre-election filing requirement?
CONCLUSION
Yes.
FACTS
You are treasurer of the San Diego County Republican Central Committee. Since
June 30, this committee has made no contributions to candidates or ballot measures, nor
has it made any independent expenditures on behalf of a candidate or ballot measure. It
has, however, spent significant funds on a member communication program.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - 9l}l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-02-262
Page No. 2
AI\ALYSIS
Payments for communications supporting or opposing a candidate or ballot
measure typically are reportable "contributions" or "expenditures" under the Political
Reform Act (the "Act").2 ($$ 82015 and82025.13 section gl3lz,enacted by
Proposition 34 and later amended by. Senate Bill34,provides an exception to this general
rule for "member communications."4
As amended, $ 85312 provides:
-.-o*,lllli"*,1'"T:f ji:; j"i:;liTffi i,?J#ffi;::3:ionsto
employees, or shareholders of an organization for the purpose of
supporting or opposing a candidate or a ballot measure are not
contributions or expenditures, provided those payments are not
made for general public advertising such as broadcasting, billboards,
and newspaper advertisements. However. payments made by a
political party for communications to its members who are
reeistered with that partv which would otherwise qualify as
contributions or expenditures shall be reported in accordance with
Article 2 (commencing with Section 84200) of chapter 4. and
Chapter 4.6 (commencing with Section 84600). of this title.,, 
s
(Emphasis added.)(Footnote added.)
Absent section 85312, the payments you describe would qualift as contributions
or expendifures. According to the last sentence of the stafute, because those palments
would qualify as such, they must be reported according to the provisions of sections
84200 et seq. and 84600 et seq. Sections 84200.5 and 84200.7, subdivision (b), require a
preelection report be filed for the November election, assuming the payment(s) for the
member communication(s) total $500 or more during the reporting period. Assuming
that threshold is met, the party must report those payments in accordance with the
provisions above even though they otherwise are payments for "member
communications."
2 All references are to the Government Code.
3 Sections 82015 and 82025 provide the definitions for conkibution and expenditure, respectively.
These sections are turther interpreted by regulations r8215(a)(l) and 1g225(a)(l).
4 'Member communications" refers to communications to members, employees, and shareholders
of an orgatization or families of those persons and is the colloquial name given to the provisions of section
8s312.
5 "'Political parfy committee' means the state central committee or count5r central committee of an
organization that meets the requirements for recognition as a political party pursuant to Section 5100 of the
Elections Code." (Section 85205.)


File No. A-02-262
Page No. 3
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660
CSTjg
I:\Advicel.trs\02-262
Sincerely,
Luisa
By:
C.
Tocher
Counsel, Legal Division

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do expenditures that constitute "mernber communications" under section 85312
by a political parfi committee nonetheless trigger a pre-election filing requirement?

CONCLUSION: Yes.
